Intro¶

Summary of the experiment:

Imaging conditions: GFP channel 10%, 100 ms exposure, no gain; same for the phluorin channel.

What this script does: plots of cellsize and fluorescence, previously stored in a dataframe (uploaded from a .pkl file). Also plots the contours and cell fluorescence determined by ColiCoords (objects also previously stored in a .pkl file)

elab entry:

Starting date of the analysis: 2023.01.30

Functions¶

In [7]:
def change_label(yy):
    if yy == "mean_gfp":
        out = r"Mean GFP fluorescence [a.u.]"
    elif yy == "mean_phluorin":
        out = r"Mean pHluorin fluorescence [a.u.]"
    elif yy == "ratio":
        out = r"$R_{400/480}$"
    elif yy == "ratio_corr":
        out = r"$R_{400/480, corrected}$"
    elif yy == "length_mj":
        out = r"Cell length [$\mu m$]"
    elif yy == "width_mj":
        out = r"Cell width [$\mu m$]"
    elif yy == "width":
        out = r"Cell width [$\mu m$]"
    elif yy == "area_mj":
        out = r"Cell area [$\mu m^2$]"
    elif yy == "mu":
        out = r"$\mu$ [$h^{-1}$]"
    elif yy == "rfp_norm":
        out = r"Normalized fluorescence [$\mu m^{-1}$]"
    elif yy == "D_mean":
        out = r"$D$ $[\mu m^2 /s]$"
    elif yy == "csource":
        out = r"Condition"
    elif yy == "pH_ext":
        out = r"$pH_{ext}$"
    elif yy == "subpopulation":
        out = "Subpopulation"
    elif yy == "mean_logD":
        out = r"Mean $log(D)$"
    elif yy == "pH":
        out = r"$pH$"
    elif yy == "t":
        out = r"Time [hh:mm]"
    elif yy == "X_at_sampling":
        out = r"Cell conc. at sampling [Z/mL]"
    else:
        out = yy
    return out
In [8]:
def plot_histogram(data, yy, hh, bins, col=None, row=None, order=None, kde=True, aspect=1.5, vertical_estimator=None, **kwargs):
    g = sns.FacetGrid(data,  
                      hue=hh,
                      hue_order=order,
                      col=col,
                      row=row,
                      sharex=True, sharey=False,      
                      height=5, despine=False, aspect=aspect, **kwargs)  

    g.map(sns.distplot, 
          yy,
          bins=bins,
          kde=kde)

    if vertical_estimator is not None:
        g.map(_plot_vertical_line, yy, estimator=vertical_estimator)

    g.add_legend()
    g.set(xlabel=change_label(yy))
    
    return g



def plot_swarm(data, yys, xx, hh, order=None, figsize=(15,5)):
    
    f, ax = plt.subplots(1, len(yys), figsize=figsize)

    for i, yy in enumerate(yys):
        g = sns.swarmplot(data=data, 
                          ax=ax[i],
                          x=xx, 
                          y=yy, 
                          order=order, 
                          hue_order=order,
                          hue=hh, 
                          size=2,  
                          alpha=0.8)

    #     g = sns.pointplot(data=df.loc[(df[yy] < cc)], ax=ax[i],
    #                      x="csource", y=yy, order=order, hue_order=order, estimator=np.mean, ci="sd", capsize=0.2, hue="Condition")

        g.set(ylabel=change_label(yy))
        g.set(xlabel=change_label(xx))

        ax[i].legend([],[], frameon=False)  
        
    return


def plot_regression(data, xx, yy, col, hh, col_order, hue_order, ):
    g = sns.lmplot(data=data,
                   x=xx, y=yy, 
                   col=col, 
                   hue=hh, 
                   hue_order=hue_order, 
                   col_order=col_order,
                   sharey=True,
                   scatter_kws={"s": 80, "edgecolor": "black", "alpha": 0.3},)

    g.set_axis_labels(change_label(xx), change_label(yy))
    g.despine(top=False, right=False)
    return g



def _plot_vertical_line(x, estimator=np.mean, **kwargs):
    """
    To map the plotting of a vertical line to a FacetGrid.
    The statistic to be plotted is defind by the inut argument <estimator>.
    """
    plt.axvline(estimator(x), linestyle="--", **kwargs)
    return
In [9]:
def gaussian(x, height, center, width, offset=0):
    """
    Returns a Gaussian function.
    """
    return height*np.exp(-(x - center)**2/(2*width**2)) + offset


def two_gaussians(x, h1, c1, w1, h2, c2, w2, offset=0):
    """
    Returns the sum of two Gaussian functions.
    """
    return (gaussian(x, h1, c1, w1, offset=0) + gaussian(x, h2, c2, w2, offset=0) + offset)


def error_function_2gauss(p, x, y):
    return (two_gaussians(x, *p) - y)**2


def fit_2gauss(errorfunction, x, y, param_guess):
    return optimize.leastsq(errorfunction, param_guess, args=(x, y))


def obtain_bins(data, bins=50):
    """
    Obtains the centers of each bin of the input data. The latter is binned according the number specified in the arguments.
    """
    values, bin_edges = np.histogram(data, bins=bins)
    bin_center =  bin_edges[:-1] + (bin_edges[1:] - bin_edges[:-1])/2
    return bin_center, values


def plot_curves(x, params_fit, ax):
    params_1 = params_fit[[0, 1, 2, 6]]
    params_2 = params_fit[[3, 4, 5, 6]]
    
    ax.plot(x, gaussian(x, *params_2), linestyle="--",) #  c=p[0].get_color()
    ax.plot(x, gaussian(x, *params_1),  linestyle="--",)

    ax.plot(x, two_gaussians(x, *params_fit),)
    return


def obtain_fractions(params_fit, int_min=0, int_max=100):
    """
    Compute the fraction of each subpopulation as the fraction of the obtained peak heights multiplied by the standard deviation.
    """
#     args_1 = tuple(params_fit[0:3])
#     args_2 = tuple(params_fit[3:6])
    
#     I1, _ = quad(gaussian, int_min, int_max, args=args_1)
#     I2, _ = quad(gaussian, int_min, int_max, args=args_2)
    h1 = params_fit[0]*np.abs(params_fit[2])
    h2 = params_fit[3]*np.abs(params_fit[5])
    f1 = h1 / (h1 + h2)
    f2 = h2 / (h1 + h2)
    
    return (f1, f2)

In [11]:
df = pd.concat([df_exp1, df_exp2, df_exp3])   

df["mean_phluorin"] = df["mean_phluorin"] - 500
df["mean_gfp"] = df["mean_gfp"] - 500

df["ratio"] = df["mean_phluorin"] / df["mean_gfp"]


df["condition"] = df["Strain"] + "-" + df["Condition"]
df = df.loc[df.type_mj=="single"]

0. Determine corrected $R_{400/480}$ for GlcCAA¶

0.1. Label WT and WT+pHluorin cells¶

In [13]:
# Confirm removal of the WT cells:
plt.hist(df.loc[df.condition=="WT-GlcCAA", "mean_phluorin"], bins=50)
plt.xlabel(change_label("mean_phluorin"))
# plt.axvline(threshold, color="red")

In [14]:
# Filter out the true WT cells (without pNTR-SD-pHluorin, in the GlcCAA condition only):
threshold = 40

df["contains_pHluorin"] = "yes"
df.loc[df["mean_gfp"] < threshold, "contains_pHluorin"] = "no"
# df = df.loc[df.contains_pHluorin == "yes"]
In [15]:
# Confirm removal of the WT cells:
plt.hist(df.loc[df.condition=="WT-GlcCAA", "mean_phluorin"], bins=50)
plt.axvline(threshold, color="red")
plt.xlabel(change_label("mean_phluorin"))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(r"{}comparison_repeats\\threshold-{}-WT-vs-pHluorin_WT-GlcCAA.png".format(path_out, threshold))
plt.show()

0.2. Correction factor¶

$$ R_{400,480}^* = \frac{I_{400, pH} - I_{400, WT}}{I_{480, pH} - I_{480, WT}} = \frac{I_{400, pH} - \alpha I_{400, pH}}{I_{480, pH} - \beta I_{480, pH}} = \frac{I_{400, pH}(1 - \alpha)}{I_{480, pH}(a - \beta)} = \frac{I_{400, pH}}{I_{480, pH}} \left(\frac{1 - \alpha}{1 - \beta} \right)$$
In [16]:
df["ratio_corr"] = df["ratio"]

5 rows × 22 columns

In [17]:
for rep, dfg in df.groupby("repeat"):
    df_caa_wt = dfg.loc[(dfg.condition=="WT-GlcCAA") & (dfg.contains_pHluorin=="no")]
    df_caa_pH = dfg.loc[(dfg.condition=="WT-GlcCAA") &  (dfg.contains_pHluorin=="yes")]
    
    autofluorescence = df_caa_wt.mean() / df_caa_pH.mean()
    
    alpha = autofluorescence["mean_phluorin"]
    beta = autofluorescence["mean_gfp"]
    
    correction_factor = (1 - alpha)/(1 - beta)
    print(rep, correction_factor)
    
    df.loc[(df.repeat==rep) & (df.condition=="WT-GlcCAA") & (df.contains_pHluorin=="yes"), "ratio_corr"] = correction_factor * df.loc[(df.repeat==rep) & (df.condition=="WT-GlcCAA") & (df.contains_pHluorin=="yes"), "ratio_corr"]

rep2 0.9579935547187984
rep3 0.9553372953286258

In [ ]:

1.4. Inspecting the absolute fluorescence values¶

In [28]:
f, ax = plt.subplots(1,3, figsize=(20,5))

for i, yy in enumerate(["mean_gfp", "mean_phluorin", "ratio"]):
    sns.pointplot(data=df, x="condition", y=yy, hue="repeat", hue_order=["rep3", "rep2", "rep1"], order=order, ax=ax[i])
    ax[i].set_ylabel(change_label(yy))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("{}comparison_repeats\mean_intensity-and-ratio_across-conditions.png".format(path_out))
plt.show()
